source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a bolted/adhesive-anchored steel baseplate assembly that anchors a tubular steel column to a cracked C20/25 normal-weight concrete slab. The assembly must be modeled as an explicit multi-body system containing: (A) one rectangular S355 steel baseplate 300 x 300 mm, at least 20 mm thick, with four 18 mm anchor clearance holes on a 200 x 200 mm square pattern; (B) one welded S355 RHS 120 x 120 x 8 column stub, 300 mm tall, centered on the baseplate; (C) four M16 grade 8.8 threaded rod anchors bonded into the cracked C20/25 concrete with an epoxy-acrylate adhesive at an effective embedment h_ef = 125 mm (catalog canonical size); (D) four hex nuts + washers at the top of the anchor rods providing baseplate clamp-up; (E) fillet weld fillets at the column-to-baseplate interface.

The representative published characteristic resistances for this configuration in cracked C20/25 concrete per representative 2024 ETA data are: characteristic tension (bond/pull-out combined) N_Rk = 74 kN per anchor, characteristic steel tension N_Rk,s = 90.4 kN per anchor, characteristic steel shear V_Rk,s = 54.2 kN per anchor, with characteristic concrete cone N_Rk,c depending on spacing and edge distance. The anchor pattern shall maintain edge distance c >= 80 mm (>= 0.5 h_ef per ETA minimum) and spacing s >= 100 mm. Anchor holes are drilled oversize by 2 mm relative to anchor diameter.

Applied loads at the top of the column stub: axial tension up to 120 kN, horizontal shear up to 80 kN, and an overturning moment of 25 kN*m about the strong axis. Pass/fail is evaluated under the ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 / EOTA TR 029 framework: (R1) per-anchor tension demand <= phi * N_Rk / gamma_M with phi = 0.75 and gamma_M,p = 1.5 for adhesive bond, giving factored tension capacity N_Rd ~= 37 kN per anchor; (R2) per-anchor shear demand <= V_Rd,s = V_Rk,s / gamma_M,s with gamma_M,s = 1.25, giving V_Rd,s ~= 43 kN per anchor; (R3) combined tension+shear interaction (N/N_Rd)^5/3 + (V/V_Rd)^5/3 <= 1.0 at every anchor; (R4) concrete breakout and pryout checks must satisfy the same gamma_M = 1.5 with the effective anchor group area A_Nc/A_Nc0; (R5) baseplate bending stress <= 0.9 * f_y = 319.5 MPa under the factored load combination (AISC 360-22 LRFD flexural resistance); (R6) plate deflection at the column footprint <= L/300 = 1.0 mm across the 300 mm plate width; (R_asm1) connection DCR for every bolted/bonded interface <= 1.0; (R_asm2) fillet weld between column stub and baseplate shall meet AISC 360-22 weld strength with DCR <= 1.0 under the factored load combination.

Manufacturing: baseplate is flame-cut then machined flat to within 0.5 mm across the column footprint; holes drilled oversize by 2 mm relative to anchor diameter; fillet welds between column stub and baseplate shop-welded per AWS D1.1. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model (baseplate + column stub + welds + four anchor rod bodies + four nut/washer bodies) with contact at the baseplate-to-concrete interface, preloaded bond-anchor stiffness, and explicit fillet-weld fillets. Per-part stress is reported for each part family but assembly-level passing requires all interface DCRs and assembly-scope checks to pass simultaneously.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly file with distinct solid bodies for baseplate, column stub, weld fillets, four anchor rod envelopes, and four nut+washer bodies, each tagged with part_family and instance_index metadata; a loads/capacities report listing per-anchor demand vs capacity, combined interaction, concrete cone check, plate stress, plate deflection, per-part max stress, and assembly-level connection DCR.
short_prompt:
Design a bolted/adhesive-anchored steel baseplate assembly anchoring a tubular steel column into a cracked C20/25 normal-weight concrete slab, delivered as an explicit multi-body system. The plate is S355 steel 300 x 300 mm (>= 20 mm thick) with four 18 mm anchor clearance holes on a 200 x 200 mm pattern; the column is a welded S355 RHS 120 x 120 x 8 stub 300 mm tall; four M16 grade 8.8 threaded rods are bonded into the concrete with an epoxy-acrylate adhesive at h_ef = 125 mm. Edge distance c >= 80 mm, spacing s >= 100 mm, holes oversized by 2 mm.

Applied at the top of the column stub: axial tension up to 120 kN, horizontal shear up to 80 kN, overturning moment 25 kN*m about the strong axis.

Governing standard ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 + EOTA TR 029 + AISC 360-22 with phi = 0.75, gamma_M,p = 1.5 for bond, gamma_M,s = 1.25 for steel. Representative characteristics: N_Rk = 74 kN, N_Rk,s = 90.4 kN, V_Rk,s = 54.2 kN, giving N_Rd ~= 37 kN and V_Rd,s ~= 43 kN per anchor. Combined interaction (N/N_Rd)^5/3 + (V/V_Rd)^5/3 <= 1.0. Plate bending stress <= 0.9 * f_y = 319.5 MPa; plate deflection <= L/300 = 1.0 mm across the 300 mm plate.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Concrete cone breakout, pry-out, and combined interaction are closed-form equations from ACI 318-19 Ch.17 / EOTA TR 029, not FEA. Standard implementations are available in anchor-design software packages.

prompt.title: Adhesive-anchor steel baseplate on C20/25 concrete (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part assembly: S355 baseplate + welded RHS column stub + 4 adhesive anchor rods + 4 nut/washer sets + fillet welds, anchoring a tubular column to cracked C20/25 concrete under combined axial, shear, and moment. Final FEM check runs on the assembled model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

parts.name: steel_baseplate
parts.role: distributes column forces into the anchor group and concrete bearing surface

parts.name: rhs_column_stub
parts.role: short welded tubular column stub that delivers service load into the baseplate via fillet welds

parts.name: adhesive_anchor_rod
parts.role: M16 grade 8.8 threaded rod bonded into cracked C20/25 concrete with epoxy-acrylate adhesive at h_ef = 125 mm

parts.name: nut_and_washer
parts.role: hex nut + washer at the top of each anchor rod providing baseplate clamp-up

parts.name: fillet_weld
parts.role: shop-welded fillet weld between column stub and baseplate per AWS D1.1
parts.material_hint: AWS D1.1 filler compatible with S355

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: approximately 300 x 300 x 320 mm (baseplate plan + column stub height)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Z up along column axis; XY in baseplate plane with anchor pattern 200 x 200 mm centered

boundary_conditions.concrete_interface: baseplate bearing on concrete; uplift resisted only by anchors
boundary_conditions.anchor_preload: catalog value per adhesive-anchor design software for M16 threaded rod
boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: each anchor rod modeled with axial + shear bond stiffness consistent with the adhesive bond + rod cross-section; nuts/washers coupled to rod top and bearing on baseplate top face

objective.minimize: baseplate mass subject to passing all anchor + plate + assembly-scope checks

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: N_Rd = N_Rk / gamma_M,p = 74 / 1.5 * phi with phi=0.75 and gamma_M,p=1.5 per representative ETA + ACI 318-19 Ch.17
pass_fail_criteria.note: Representative factored capacity for adhesive anchor + M16 h_ef=125 mm, cracked C20/25.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: group N_Rd,c with A_Nc/A_Nc0 per ACI 318-19 17.4.2
L/300 with L = 300 mm plate span
assembly-scope demand/capacity ratio on every bolted/bonded interface under LC1
AISC 360-22 Chapter J weld strength under factored load combination
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

requirements.governing_standard: ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 + EOTA TR 029 + AISC 360-22 + representative adhesive-anchor ETA

interfaces.load_path: column axial + shear + moment into baseplate via fillet weld
baseplate uplift reacted by anchor rods bonded in concrete
nut/washer clamp on baseplate top face transferring preload

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Baseplate, welded column stub, four adhesive anchor rods, four nut/washer sets, and fillet weld are distinct bodies with distinct load paths that cannot be represented as a single solid body.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] are inferred from the original prompt; nut/washer and weld fillet bodies are speculative modeling artifacts included to make the assembly explicit.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
